entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop that releases biological nitrification inhibitors from its roots to manage nitrogen in the soil

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITRIFICATION INHIBITORS
entities.description: Natural compounds released by plant roots that slow down the conversion of ammonium to nitrate by soil bacteria

entities.name: HYDROPHILIC-BNIS
entities.description: Water-soluble biological nitrification inhibitors whose release is influenced by rhizosphere pH and H+ATPase activity

entities.name: HYDROPHOBIC-BNIS
entities.description: Fat-soluble biological nitrification inhibitors whose release is not affected by rhizosphere pH

entities.name: RHIZOSPHERE PH
entities.description: The pH level of the soil environment immediately surrounding the roots, which regulates the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: PLASMA MEMBRANE H+ATPASE
entities.description: A proton pump in the root cell membrane that is stimulated by low pH and linked to the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: HYDROPHONIC SYSTEM
entities.description: A soil-less cultivation method used to control the pH of nutrient solutions for sorghum plants

entities.name: FUSICOCCIN
entities.description: A pharmacological agent that promotes H+ extrusion and stimulates the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: VANADATE
entities.description: A pharmacological agent that suppresses H+ extrusion and lowers the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: NIFLUMIC ACID
entities.description: An anion-channel blocker that enhances H+ extrusion and the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: ANTHRACENE-9-CARBOXYLATE
entities.description: An anion-channel blocker that enhances H+ extrusion and the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: NITROSOMONAS EUROPAEA BIOASSAY
entities.description: A luminescent assay using Nitrosomonas europaea to measure the activity of biological nitrification inhibitors

entities.name: SORGOLEONE
entities.description: A hydrophobic compound in sorghum root exudates that correlates positively with hydrophobic-BNI activity

entities.name: H+ EXCRETION
entities.description: The process of releasing protons from roots into the rhizosphere, closely coupled with the release of hydrophilic-BNIs

entities.name: TWO-PHASE PARTITIONING SYSTEM
entities.description: A method used to isolate the plasma membrane from root tissues to determine H+ ATPase activity

entities.name: NITROSOMONAS EUROPAEA
entities.description: An ammonia-oxidizing bacterium used as the biological indicator in the BNI activity bioassay

entities.name: ANION-CHANNEL BLOCKERS
entities.description: Chemical agents that inhibit anion channels, leading to increased H+ extrusion and hydrophilic-BNI release

entities.name: MEMBRANE TRANSPORTERS
entities.description: Unknown proteins in the root membrane that operate the release of protonated BNIs to maintain charge balance
